data.title: Australian Shares Gain for 7th Straight Session
data.description:
The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.22% to close at 8,298 on Thursday, extending its winning streak to a seventh session as gains in the financial and consumer sectors outweighed losses among commodity-linked stocks.

Investor sentiment was buoyed by a strong labor market report, showing the economy added 89,000 net new jobs in April—far surpassing the forecast of 20,000 and pushing total employment to a record 14.64 million.

The upbeat data lent support to the domestic growth outlook.

Despite expectations that the Reserve Bank of Australia will lower the cash rate by 25 basis points to 3.85% at its meeting next week, traders have scaled back bets on deeper policy easing.

Markets now anticipate around 76 basis points of total cuts by year-end, down from 100 basis points just weeks ago.

Top performers included Commonwealth Bank (+1.2%), Macquarie Group (+0.8%), ANZ Group (+1.7%), Aristocrat Leisure (+2.1%), and Wesfarmers (+2.2%).

data.title: A producer from China offers High impact polystyrene (HIPS) in Bangladesh
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Bangladesh informed that a Chinese producer is heard to have announced its offer for HIPS grade at the USD 1170/mt levels. This offer is for end May/early June 2025 shipment on CFR Chattogram port basis.

data.title: India's Remsons Industries jumps on 3 bln-rupee order win
data.description:
** Remsons Industries jumps ~12% to 134.17 rupees, highest in nearly four months

** Automotive equipment maker bags 3 bln-rupee ($35 mln) order, its largest business win to date, from the North American division of carmaker Stellantis

** Order size as much as 64% of REIN's market cap and nearly as much as co's FY24 revenue

** Co did not disclose length of partnership

** REIN trims 12-month decline to 31.1%

Eurozone Bond Yields Open Marginally Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields edge marginally higher after market opening, awaiting domestic and U.S. data. In the U.S., retail sales will be the main highlight after a strong March, while the PPI data will be watched after the cooler-than-expected CPI, says ING's rates strategist team. In the eurozone, industrial production data will be released alongside the second reading of first-quarter GDP growth. The 10-year German Bund yield is up 1 basis point at 2.694%, while the 10-year Italian-German yield spread remains at 100 basis points, the tightest level since 2021, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)
